mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals broad economic expansion, directly boosting corporate earnings expectations and investor confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day S&P 500 return rank IC with PAYEMS change falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or correlation is negative in expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 9.5% and remains elevated, it indicates severe labor market slack that depresses consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when trigger is active, or 60-day forward return IC with UNRATE level is above -0.05 across triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of an accommodative fed funds rate (FEDFUNDS near zero) and strong retail sales (RSAFS) growth signals effective monetary transmission and robust consumer demand, amplifying positive equity returns over a one-month horizon beyond what either series alone would suggest.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (FEDFUNDS * RSAFS growth) with 20-day SPX returns is below 0.04 over any 2-year walk-forward period, or the interaction does not improve upon single-series models (MSE increase).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, rising industrial production (INDPRO) above the index baseline (100) signals supply-side bounce-back and capacity utilization improvement, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as output gaps close.
failure_condition: When INDPRO > 100, the proportion of positive 60-day SPX returns is below 50% or the mean return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) across all such episodes in the walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) reflect strengthening construction activity and residential investment, which provides a near-term boost to equity markets through positive wealth effects and improved sentiment over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The rank IC of HOUST month-over-month change with 5-day SPX forward returns is below 0.02 over any consecutive 12-month period, or the signal becomes inverted (negative correlation) in expansionary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year exceeds 3%, it triggers expectations of monetary tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, the average 20-day SPX return is positive or the Rank IC of the trigger indicator with returns is above 0.00, indicating no consistent negative predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in both nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) creates a self-reinforcing demand cycle: employment gains boost income, which fuels consumption, driving S&P 500 returns higher over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction signal (both rising) fails to produce positive median 60-day SPX returns, or the hit rate (proportion of positive returns) is below 55% across all triggered windows in the walk-forward set.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a rising fed funds rate (FEDFUNDS) increases the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, reducing present values and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month. This mechanism is only active when the regime is defined as an active tightening sequence.
failure_condition: When the trigger is active during tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS rising), the average 20-day SPX return is not negative, or the predictive sign flips to positive in more than 30% of such episodes.
